post_content: Provincial governing body for archery in PEI. President Duncan Crawford. Hosts provincial championships, Atlantic championships, and national events. Offers Game On program for girls ages 14-18. Duncan Crawford is Head Coach of National Paralympic Archery Team. Active and hosting events through 2025-2026.

Elite archery training centre owned by Duncan Crawford. Rebuilt after 2017 fire. Approximately 70 members. Indoor range with 8+ targets (plans to expand to 16). Hosted 2018 PEI Provincial Championships and 2022 Canadian Outdoor Championships (3D and Field). Only certified archery facility of its kind in Canada. 6-week beginner courses available with nationally certified coach. Max 8 students per instructor. All equipment provided. Sport tax receipts issued.

Family operation offering horse archery experiences. Emma and Marina are CFMA certified instructors. Ground archery $75/hr (1-2 people) or $50/hr (3+). Mounted archery $75/hr (2hr min). Iron Horse Archers club $60/session (3.5hrs) on 1st and 3rd Saturdays May onwards. Group experiences $500 half-day / $1000 full-day (max 9 people). Equestrian instruction $50-75/hr. All equipment and horses provided.

POSSIBLY CLOSED. Archery equipment business established by Jianan Zhang in 2014. Imported competitively priced archery equipment parts from China. Company dissolved February 2018. Certificate of revival issued April 2018 but no evidence of current operations. No phone, email, or website found.
